title: Agent Knowledge Sandbox: Curated Sources + Graph Memory
decisions: Snapshot-backed graph storage is the current shipped backend; Kuzu-WASM is deferred
Temporal edges with Graphiti validity windows
No LLM during retrieval (hard perf requirement)
Vellum material language for all UI surfaces
Entity extraction as new skill using existing cascade
Memory provenance and confirmation labels are part of the product contract: observed, inferred, user-confirmed, imported, and stale memories must behave differently in retrieval and UI.
Vector retrieval is implemented over persisted entity embeddings; Kuzu-WASM remains deferred.

Context-graph memory v1 stays browser-native: member-private source bodies and reasoning traces remain local, while shared coop projection is limited to approved artifacts, source registry metadata, charter fields, and validated insights.
Coop memory charter is member-editable shared state derived at bootstrap from purpose and setup insights; the agent reads it but must not rewrite it automatically.
notes: Context-graph memory upgrade is active on top of the closed sandbox baseline. This pass keeps snapshot-backed Dexie graph storage, adds a bootstrap memory charter, persists fetched source bodies locally as encrypted source content, wires source-content observations to content IDs, carries graph context and precedent traces into prompts/traces, syncs shared-safe source registry metadata through Yjs, and keeps raw source bodies, raw captures, prompts, and unconfirmed graph facts out of shared coop state. The focused local QA gate is now `bun run test:unit:memory-loop` / `bun run validate memory-loop`, with the manual Browser/Computer Use guide in `eval/local-functional-qa.md`.
